title: Text has parts
checkLabels.t6: The length is not typed in
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: Five lines in the console, four of them wrong. Look at them one at a time.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.erased-source: You removed the spaces from the typed text.
hint2.erased-source: That is how people type into a field. The task is for the code to cope with it, not to pretend it does not happen.
hint2.typed-answer: It is counted from the text itself. Tomorrow the order will be different.
why.not-trimmed: The spaces at the front and back are still there.
hint2.not-trimmed: There is a method that removes spaces at both ends and leaves the ones in the middle alone.
hint3.not-trimmed: Methods are called with a dot and brackets after the name. Without the brackets nothing happens.
hint2.wrong-size: Flat White is ten characters including the space between the words. If you are counting something else, you have not removed the outer spaces yet.
why.wrong-initial: The first letter is not F.
hint2.wrong-initial: Counting starts at zero, not one.
hint3.wrong-initial: Position 1 is the second letter. The first is at position 0.
why.not-called: The capitals do not come out. The function itself does.
hint2.not-called: You forgot the brackets. Without them you are not asking for the result — you are asking for the method itself.
hint3.not-called: This is a quiet mistake: nothing breaks, but instead of text you get something you cannot use.
why.wrong-includes: The latte check does not answer correctly.
hint2.wrong-includes: Flat White is not a latte. If you get something else, you are comparing the wrong things.
hint3.wrong-includes: Searching tells capitals and lowercase apart. "Latte" and "latte" are different to it.
hint4.wrong-includes: Bring the text down to lowercase before searching. Methods chain: first one, then the other.
why.wrong-count: There are not five lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Somebody typed their order into a field. They typed it with spaces at the front and back, because that is what people do.
blocks.1.text: Five lines process that text. Four of the five return something that is not what it should be — and not one of them breaks.
blocks.3.text: Every piece of text knows how long it is and has an address for each letter. Counting starts at zero.
blocks.5.text: The length is 4. Position 0 is B, position 3 is n. Position 4 does not exist and returns undefined, with no error.
blocks.7.text: Text has ready-made actions — remove the spaces, make it capitals, look for something inside. They are written with a dot after the name.
blocks.8.text: And always with brackets at the end. The brackets mean do it. Without them you get the action itself, unperformed.
blocks.9.text: This is one of the few mistakes nothing gives away. The result is something that looks meaningless, and the code carries on running.
blocks.11.text: Every method returns a new piece of text. So you can call the next one on it, and another. It is written one after the other, left to right.
blocks.12.text: The original never changes. String methods always make something new and leave the old one as it was.
blocks.14.text: Fix the five lines. Do not touch what the person typed.
blocks.15.items.0: remove the spaces from both ends
blocks.15.items.3: the latte check should work with capitals too
